summary: This week's AI news highlights significant investments and advancements in the field, with Google and OpenAI leading the charge. AI spending and infrastructure development are on the rise, with a focus on responsible energy and community investment. Meanwhile, concerns over AI's energy bill and cybersecurity threats continue to grow.
insight: The increasing adoption of AI is driving innovation and investment, but also raises important questions about the technology's impact on society and the environment, highlighting the need for responsible development and deployment of AI systems.
highlights: Google justifies its massive AI spending with a booming cloud business
OpenAI's AI spending spree has ballooned to $750B
AMD commits up to $5 billion to Anthropic, expanding the AI company's computing power

articles.title: Google justifies its massive AI spending with a booming cloud business
articles.description: Google's cloud business is thriving, as companies adopting its AI and AI infrastructure services help the tech giant to report record profits.

articles.title: OpenAI’s AI spending spree has ballooned to $750B
articles.description: OpenAI will spend the equivalent of Sweden's GDP on infrastructure through 2030.

articles.title: AMD commits up to $5 billion to  Anthropic

articles.title: Travis Kalanick’s robotics company raises $1.7B, led by a16z
articles.description: Uber is also investing in Travis Kalanick's company Atoms, which has made gauzy claims about using industrial AI to modernize the world.

articles.title: After shocking quarter, IBM insists that AI isn’t killing the mainframe
articles.description: After IBM's stock crashed last week on warnings of poor mainframe sales, the CEO explained that AI wrecked corporate hardware budget, temporarily.

articles.title: How OpenAI’s human mistake led to the AI-powered hack on Hugging Face

articles.description: "This is day one for cybersecurity in the age of agents," Hugging Face CEO says.

articles.title: Accelerating the frontiers of scientific discovery: Google’s $40M commitment to the Genesis Mission
articles.description: Google commits $40M in AI tokens and credits for the Genesis Mission

articles.title: Google announces Gemini 3.6 Flash and cybersecurity AI, teases 3.5 Pro and Gemini 4
articles.description: There are new 3.6 and 3.5 models today, but Google is already training Gemini 4.
